title: Infer requirements from tests and complete stub implementation
goal: Complete the implementation in models.py and scheduler.py to pass all tests in tests.py. The tests define the expected behavior — read them carefully to understand what each method should do.
hints: The test file is your specification. Read all tests before implementing. Pay attention to edge cases in tests like duplicate detection, dependency chain unblocking, and cycle detection.

seed_files.tests.py:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from models import Task, TaskBoard, Priority, PRIORITIES

# ---- TaskBoard tests ----

def test_add_task():
    board = TaskBoard("Test")
    t = Task("Buy milk", PRIORITIES[3])
    board.add_task(t)
    assert len(board.tasks) == 1
    assert board.tasks[0].title == "Buy milk"

def test_add_duplicate_raises():
    board = TaskBoard("Test")
    board.add_task(Task("Buy milk", PRIORITIES[3]))
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="duplicate"):
        board.add_task(Task("Buy milk", PRIORITIES[1]))

def test_complete_task():
    board = TaskBoard("Test")
    board.add_task(Task("Buy milk", PRIORITIES[3]))
    result = board.complete_task("Buy milk")
    assert result is True
    assert board.tasks[0].is_completed

def test_complete_nonexistent():
    board = TaskBoard("Test")
    result = board.complete_task("Ghost")
    assert result is False

def test_complete_unblocks_dependents():
    board = TaskBoard("Test")
    board.add_task(Task("Write code", PRIORITIES[2]))
    board.add_task(Task("Review code", PRIORITIES[2], blocked_by=["Write code"]))
    board.add_task(Task("Deploy", PRIORITIES[1], blocked_by=["Review code"]))
    board.complete_task("Write code")
    review = [t for t in board.tasks if t.title == "Review code"][0]
    assert not review.is_blocked
    deploy = [t for t in board.tasks if t.title == "Deploy"][0]
    assert deploy.is_blocked  # still blocked by Review code

def test_get_by_priority():
    board = TaskBoard("Test")
    board.add_task(Task("A", PRIORITIES[1]))
    board.add_task(Task("B", PRIORITIES[3]))
    board.add_task(Task("C", PRIORITIES[1]))
    result = board.get_by_priority(1)
    assert len(result) == 2
    assert all(t.priority.level == 1 for t in result)

def test_get_blocked():
    board = TaskBoard("Test")
    board.add_task(Task("A", PRIORITIES[1]))
    board.add_task(Task("B", PRIORITIES[2], blocked_by=["A"]))
    blocked = board.get_blocked()
    assert len(blocked) == 1
    assert blocked[0].title == "B"

def test_get_actionable():
    board = TaskBoard("Test")
    board.add_task(Task("A", PRIORITIES[1]))
    board.add_task(Task("B", PRIORITIES[2], blocked_by=["A"]))
    board.complete_task("A")
    # A is completed, B is now unblocked
    actionable = board.get_actionable()
    assert len(actionable) == 1
    assert actionable[0].title == "B"

def test_summary():
    board = TaskBoard("Test")
    board.add_task(Task("A", PRIORITIES[1]))
    board.add_task(Task("B", PRIORITIES[2], blocked_by=["A"]))
    board.add_task(Task("C", PRIORITIES[3]))
    s = board.summary()
    assert s["total"] == 3
    assert s["completed"] == 0
    assert s["blocked"] == 1
    assert s["actionable"] == 2

# ---- Scheduler tests ----

from scheduler import Scheduler

def test_next_task_by_priority():
    board = TaskBoard("Test")
    board.add_task(Task("Low", PRIORITIES[4]))
    board.add_task(Task("High", PRIORITIES[1]))
    board.add_task(Task("Med", PRIORITIES[3]))
    s = Scheduler(board)
    assert s.next_task().title == "High"

def test_next_task_tiebreak_by_created():
    board = TaskBoard("Test")
    t1 = Task("First", PRIORITIES[2], created_at=datetime(2024, 1, 1))
    t2 = Task("Second", PRIORITIES[2], created_at=datetime(2024, 1, 2))
    board.add_task(t1)
    board.add_task(t2)
    s = Scheduler(board)
    assert s.next_task().title == "First"

def test_next_task_skips_blocked():
    board = TaskBoard("Test")
    board.add_task(Task("A", PRIORITIES[1]))
    board.add_task(Task("B", PRIORITIES[1], blocked_by=["A"]))
    s = Scheduler(board)
    assert s.next_task().title == "A"

def test_next_task_skips_completed():
    board = TaskBoard("Test")
    board.add_task(Task("A", PRIORITIES[1]))
    board.complete_task("A")
    board.add_task(Task("B", PRIORITIES[2]))
    s = Scheduler(board)
    assert s.next_task().title == "B"

def test_schedule_batch():
    board = TaskBoard("Test")
    board.add_task(Task("C", PRIORITIES[3]))
    board.add_task(Task("A", PRIORITIES[1]))
    board.add_task(Task("B", PRIORITIES[2]))
    s = Scheduler(board)
    batch = s.schedule_batch(2)
    assert len(batch) == 2
    assert batch[0].title == "A"
    assert batch[1].title == "B"

def test_schedule_batch_respects_blocked():
    board = TaskBoard("Test")
    board.add_task(Task("Blocker", PRIORITIES[3]))
    board.add_task(Task("Blocked", PRIORITIES[1], blocked_by=["Blocker"]))
    board.add_task(Task("Free", PRIORITIES[2]))
    s = Scheduler(board)
    batch = s.schedule_batch(2)
    assert len(batch) == 2
    titles = [t.title for t in batch]
    assert "Blocked" not in titles

def test_find_critical_path():
    board = TaskBoard("Test")
    board.add_task(Task("Root", PRIORITIES[1]))
    board.add_task(Task("Mid", PRIORITIES[2], blocked_by=["Root"]))
    board.add_task(Task("Leaf", PRIORITIES[3], blocked_by=["Mid"]))
    board.add_task(Task("Side", PRIORITIES[1], blocked_by=["Root"]))
    s = Scheduler(board)
    path = s.find_critical_path()
    assert path == ["Root", "Mid", "Leaf"]

def test_find_critical_path_empty():
    board = TaskBoard("Test")
    board.add_task(Task("A", PRIORITIES[1]))
    s = Scheduler(board)
    assert s.find_critical_path() == []

def test_estimate_completion_order():
    board = TaskBoard("Test")
    board.add_task(Task("Deploy", PRIORITIES[1], blocked_by=["Test"]))
    board.add_task(Task("Test", PRIORITIES[2], blocked_by=["Code"]))
    board.add_task(Task("Code", PRIORITIES[3]))
    board.add_task(Task("Design", PRIORITIES[4]))
    s = Scheduler(board)
    order = s.estimate_completion_order()
    # Code first (highest priority actionable), then Test (unblocked), then Deploy (unblocked), then Design
    assert order == ["Code", "Test", "Deploy", "Design"]

def test_detect_cycles_none():
    board = TaskBoard("Test")
    board.add_task(Task("A", PRIORITIES[1]))
    board.add_task(Task("B", PRIORITIES[2], blocked_by=["A"]))
    s = Scheduler(board)
    assert s.detect_cycles() == []

def test_detect_cycles_simple():
    board = TaskBoard("Test")
    board.add_task(Task("A", PRIORITIES[1], blocked_by=["B"]))
    board.add_task(Task("B", PRIORITIES[2], blocked_by=["A"]))
    s = Scheduler(board)
    cycles = s.detect_cycles()
    assert len(cycles) > 0
    # Should contain A-B cycle
    pairs = [(a, b) for a, b in cycles]
    assert ("A", "B") in pairs or ("B", "A") in pairs

ground_truth: Implement all TODO stubs in models.py (TaskBoard methods: add_task with duplicate check raising ValueError, complete_task that sets completed_at and removes title from blocked_by lists, get_by_priority filtering by level, get_blocked/get_actionable filtering by state, summary returning dict with total/completed/blocked/actionable counts) and scheduler.py (Scheduler methods: next_task returns highest-priority actionable task with created_at tiebreak, schedule_batch returns sorted top-n actionable, find_critical_path does DFS to find longest dependency chain, estimate_completion_order simulates sequential completion picking highest priority actionable each step, detect_cycles uses DFS to find circular blocked_by references).

eval_spec.check_script_content:
#!/bin/bash
set +e
cd "$WORKDIR"
result=$(python3 -m pytest tests.py -v --tb=short 2>&1)
echo "$result"
passed=$(echo "$result" | grep -c " PASSED")
failed=$(echo "$result" | grep -c " FAILED")
total=$((passed + failed))
echo ""
echo "Score: $passed/$total"
exit 0
